explicit_text: I was born in Chicago in 1914 and grew up on the outskirts of the city near Budlong Pickle Farm. Our neighborhood had gaslights that a man would light every evening, and the fire and police departments still used horse-drawn equipment. I lived in a house, not an apartment, and remember the mix of urban life with the nearby woods and farms.

explicit_text: My father used to play tennis every Saturday with a neighbor named Kraft, who was starting out in the cheese business. He had a wagon and would go around selling cheese in the neighborhood, which was the beginning of what became Kraft Cheese Company. explicit_text: My full name is Emerson Seville Woelffer, named partly after my mother's father, Algernon Sidney Seville. Growing up in Chicago, I had a unique family background blending German and French-Swiss heritage. My experiences and memories from childhood shaped who I am.

explicit_text: My maternal grandfather, Algernon Sidney Seville, was in the music business with QRS Piano Rolls in Memphis, Tennessee.
